summary: The FOMC confirmed the break. A widely expected rate hold read hawkish once the dot plot and Chair Warsh's first press conference landed, and the 2:00 PM decision bar broke the morning range down across the board. NQ fell 0.51% to 29,832.75 (flushing to 29,612 before a late short-cover), ES lost 0.97% and the 7,500 line, and gold dropped 1.76% and LOST its 4,338 trend stop, so the last standing uptrend is gone. VIX jumped 12.37% to 18.44. All three major legs now sit below their ATR stops. Throttle stays DEFENSIVE into the first post-event session.

setups.name: AVWAP Hand-off
setups.entry: Short continuation below AVWAP after a clean OR down-break
setups.bias: Down regime, both index stops overhead. Valid only on a clean OR down-break, not on a knee-jerk open spike. The 8:30 ET data open is a mini-event.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band
setups.entry: Reversion only on a volume-exhaustion tag of -2s / -3s
setups.bias: Post-FOMC flushes overshoot. Reversion eligible only on an exhaustion print, never on momentum into the band.

event.name: FOMC decision + SEP / dot plot (Chair Warsh's first meeting)
event.consensus: No rate change expected (~98% priced going in)
event.actual: Hold delivered at 2:00 PM ET; the tape read the dots and the new Chair as hawkish-leaning
event.surprise: Hawkish relative to a market that had drifted higher for weeks
event.nqReaction: NQ -0.51% to 29,832.75. The opening range held all morning, then the 2:00 PM decision bar broke it down; price drove to 29,612 (through the -3 sigma band) and short-covered into the cash close, finishing 29,833, still below the 30,063 flipped ATR stop.
event.crossAsset: Broad de-risk: ES -0.97% (lost the 7,500 psych), gold -1.76% and LOST its 4,338 trend stop with no haven bid, crude -1.29% after a failed 79.88 open spike. VIX +12.37% to 18.44. Equities and gold falling together points to a firmer-dollar, higher-real-yield read rather than a growth scare.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: No clean opening gap; RTH opened near the prior close, then the 2:00 PM decision drove the move. No gap-fade setup.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: The 1hr leg is firmly down and confirmed. Watch whether the longer 252 filter finally rolls over to confirm, or holds long and diverges from the short-timeframe break.

risk.ddProximity: NORMAL band; TSMOM-20 stayed flat through the event, no drawdown taken
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full (flat into and out of the decision)
risk.sizing: No new index longs until stops reclaim (MNQ 30,063, MES 7,483). Gold is no longer a trend long after losing 4,338. Crude a no-touch after the whipsaw. Reversion only on exhaustion tags, half size.
risk.note: The FOMC catalyst is now realized, so the binding constraint shifts from event exposure to expanded volatility: VIX +12.37% to 18.44 and MNQ ATR(14) up roughly 40% to 155. Keep size defensive through the first post-FOMC session, which frequently retraces the knee-jerk. Week ahead: Initial Jobless Claims and the Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index Thursday 8:30 AM ET, treat that open like a mini-event. With all three core legs (NQ, ES, GC) below their ATR stops, no trend-following long habitat exists in the stack.

psychology: The event you waited two sessions for finally arrived, and the map called it. That is exactly the trap: after a clean read, the urge is to press. But the first post-FOMC session frequently retraces the knee-jerk move, and a 40% jump in ATR means your normal stop distance now buys you half the room. Let the dust settle, trade smaller, demand a confirmed hourly close, and remember that being right about the direction is not the same as being early enough to chase it.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionDOWNTREND CONFIRMED. Tuesday's index break held and extended. MNQ (30,063) and MES (7,483) stops overhead; gold LOST its 4,338 stop and flipped short (4,322). All three major legs below their ATR stopsHigh
VolatilityEXPANDING. VIX +12.37% to 18.44, the sharpest jump of the leg. MNQ ATR(14) 110.5 to 155.3 (roughly +40%)High
SessionNY trend day DOWN, event-driven. OR held all morning; the 2:00 PM ET FOMC bar broke it down across the board. MNQ drove to 29,612 then short-covered to 29,833High
InventoryIMBALANCED, short lean easing late. Close 29,833 below AVWAP 29,993, but the bounce off the -3s flush shows short-covering into the cash closeMedium
EventPOST_EVENT. FOMC decision plus SEP and dot plot delivered (Chair Warsh's first). Reaction hawkish-leaning, risk-off. Catalyst realizedHigh
LiquidityThinner, headline-sensitive. Crude's 79.88 open spike-and-reverse and the cross-asset whipsaw show fragile event liquidity; VIX elevatedMedium

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 16 to Jun 17)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultGrade
1MNQ reclaims 30,262 flipped ATR stop on an hourly close (pre-2:00 PM)NOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Pre-2:00 PM hourly closes peaked near 30,151; the 30,262 reclaim never came.
2MNQ stays below 30,262 and loses 30,000 psych / 29,931 -2 sigma on an hourly closeTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Stayed below 30,262; the 2:00 PM bar closed 29,962 (lost 30,000), the next bar closed 29,679 and printed 29,612 (below -2s). Continuation worked.
3MNQ tags -3 sigma 29,818 on a volume-exhaustion barTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. The post-FOMC flush printed 29,612, through 29,818; price then short-covered to close 29,833, the reversion location held into the close.
4MES loses 7,500 psych / 7,510 -2 sigma on an hourly closeTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. MES closed 7,473 on the 2:00 PM bar (below 7,500), drove to 7,405. Broad risk-off confirmed NQ; both indexes stayed below their flipped stops.
5MGC loses 4,338 rising stop on an hourly closeTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Gold lost 4,338 on the FOMC bar (closed 4,305), flipped short to 4,322, fell to 4,238, closed 4,277. The last standing uptrend broke.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Strong day for the map: the FOMC drove rows 2-5. Row 2 (lost 30,000 / -2s), row 3 (tagged -3s then reverted), row 4 (MES lost 7,500), and row 5 (gold lost 4,338) all triggered and played out. Row 1 (MNQ reclaim 30,262) not triggered, neutral.
